captain_zara_morn:idle.greeting: Captain Zara Morn lowers the rifle from her shoulder and squints into the sun. "Walker. The perimeter holds today. Make it brief — the next shift is in twenty."

nodes.npcText: "You're either reinforcement, intelligence, or interruption. Pick."

playerOptions.text: Intelligence. Lattice-Crucible scouts have been probing.
Reinforcement. I'll take a patrol shift.

nodes.npcText: "I've seen them. I've not engaged. They've not engaged either. Tell me what you know and we'll work out what kind of probe this is."

playerOptions.text: Their lead scout is Emer Voss. They want a mutual perimeter watch.

nodes.npcText: She lets out a long breath. "That's better than I hoped. Bring me a token from them — anything — and I'll send one back. We can both stop sleeping with one eye open."
"Western road. Walk in pairs with Pell Orange. If you hear the bird that isn't a bird, hit the dirt and don't move until I say."
She slings the rifle back. "Watch your step."

councillor_mara_pin:idle.greeting: Councillor Pin looks up from a stack of letters. Three pencils are in her breast pocket; the desk has four. "Walker. Sit. Don't move the inkwell."

nodes.npcText: "I write letters. Some get read. I have written one hundred and seventeen this year. You will not be the recipient of any of them, but you may be the bearer of one."

playerOptions.text: I can carry one to Elder Mira Lattice in the hub.
Tell me about your correspondence with the hub.

nodes.npcText: She does not show surprise. "Then you know more than I assumed. I would be grateful. The letter is on the third pencil's pile. Hand it to her, not an aide. Do not read it."
"It is on the topic of frontier autonomy. The Assembly considers us tax-paying outposts. I consider us a partner. Mira and I are working out which framing survives the next vote."
"Walkers can help by carrying letters. Carry one to Mira. That is the help that helps."
She returns to the stack. "Quiet door, please."
